title: Sum of Array Elements
text:
Given an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10⁵), followed by N space-separated integers aᵢ (-10⁹ ≤ aᵢ ≤ 10⁹), print their sum.

Input format:
N
a₁ a₂ ... aₙ

Output format:
A single integer representing the sum of all elements.

Example:
Input:
3
1 2 3

Output:
6
starter_code:
def solve():
    n = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    # Compute and print the sum

solve()

title: Positive and Negative Count
text:
Given an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10⁵), followed by N space-separated integers aᵢ (-10⁹ ≤ aᵢ ≤ 10⁹), print the count of positive numbers and the count of negative numbers, separated by a space. Zero is neither positive nor negative.

Input format:
N
a₁ a₂ ... aₙ

Output format:
P N  (where P is count of numbers > 0, N is count of numbers < 0)

Example:
Input:
6
1 -2 3 -4 5 0

Output:
3 2
starter_code:

    n = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    # Compute and print counts of positive and negative numbers

solve()

title: Kth Smallest Element
text:
Given an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10⁵), followed by N space-separated integers aᵢ (-10⁹ ≤ aᵢ ≤ 10⁹), then an integer K (1 ≤ K ≤ N), print the Kth smallest element (1-indexed) in the array.

Input format:
N
a₁ a₂ ... aₙ
K

Output format:
A single integer.

Example:
Input:
6
7 2 9 1 5 3
3

Output:
3
(Sorted: [1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 9]; the 3rd smallest is 3.)
starter_code:
def solve():
    n = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    k = int(input())
    
    # Compute and print the kth smallest element

solve()

title: Longest Increasing Subarray
text:
Given an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10⁵), followed by N space-separated integers aᵢ (-10⁹ ≤ aᵢ ≤ 10⁹), print the length of the longest contiguous strictly increasing subarray.

Input format:
N
a₁ a₂ ... aₙ

Output format:
A single integer.

Example:
Input:
6
1 2 3 2 5 7

Output:
3
(The longest strictly increasing subarray is [1, 2, 3] of length 3. [2, 5, 7] also has length 3.)
starter_code:

    n = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    # Compute and print the length of the longest increasing subarray

solve()

title: Maximum Subarray Sum
text:
Given an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10⁵), followed by N space-separated integers aᵢ (-10⁴ ≤ aᵢ ≤ 10⁴), find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray. Print a single integer — the maximum subarray sum.

Input format:
N
a₁ a₂ ... aₙ

Output format:
A single integer.

Example:

-2 1 -3 4 -1 2 1 -5

Output:
6
(The contiguous subarray [4, -1, 2, 1] has the largest sum = 6.)

    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    # Compute and print the maximum subarray sum

solve()
